channel.title: Policy & Security Think Tank Updates
channel.description: Custom RSS feed from CSIS, CFR, and other policy institutions

item.title: States Weekly: March 11, 2026
item.description: This edition of States Weekly covers March 11, 2026.

item.title: The Wars After the War: Why Israel and Iran May Keep Fighting
item.description: Even after the latest war with Iran ends, lower-level hostilities between Iran and Israel—and perhaps the United States—might continue.

item.title: What Is at Stake in Hungary’s Election?
item.description: Hungary’s April election could end Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule as opposition leader Peter Magyar mounts a serious challenge. The outcome will shape Hungary’s democratic trajectory and impact the European Union’s unity and policy for years to come.

item.title: Iran’s Real War Is Against the Global Economy
item.description: EST President Navin Girishankar argues that Iran may be losing the military contest with the United States, but it is fighting a different war—one aimed at the global economy.
